title: LinkedIn CPL holds flat while March climbed 12%
anchor: April CPL sits at $138 versus March at $156, with Q1 average at $174.
connect: April 1 audience refresh hit 4 campaigns covering 62% of paid budget.
body: April CPL sits at $138 versus March at $156, with Q1 average at $174. April 1 audience refresh hit 4 campaigns covering 62% of paid budget.
trace: CPL efficiency improved despite platform inflation trends, audience refresh timing correlates with the improvement.

title: Paid pipeline pacing hits $1.18M by week 3 of April
anchor: April pipeline at $1,180,000 of the $1,500,000 monthly target through 2026-04-21.
connect: March inbound demo requests ran at 61 versus April at 84.
body: April pipeline at $1,180,000 of the $1,500,000 monthly target through 2026-04-21. March inbound demo requests ran at 61 versus April at 84.
trace: Paid pipeline ahead of pace with 10 days left in month, inbound demo volume up 38% supporting the acceleration.

title: Webinar campaign generates 312 MQLs in 11 days
anchor: April 9 webinar produced 42% of April's total 740 MQLs, concentrated in mid-market.
connect: Webinar MQL-to-SQL conversion runs at 19% versus paid social at 11%.
body: April 9 webinar produced 42% of April's total 740 MQLs, concentrated in mid-market. Webinar MQL-to-SQL conversion runs at 19% versus paid social at 11%.
trace: Single webinar campaign dominated monthly MQL production with higher conversion quality than other channels.

title: Pricing models post moves from position 7 to position 2
anchor: SaaS pricing models keyword jumped in 9 days ending 2026-04-21, with 4,400 monthly search volume.
connect: Post refresh happened 2026-04-12, FAQ section added in March update.
body: SaaS pricing models keyword jumped in 9 days ending 2026-04-21, with 4,400 monthly search volume. Post refresh happened 2026-04-12, FAQ section added in March update.
trace: Major keyword ranking improvement following content refresh, FAQ sections appear to be working for search performance.

title: Three keywords hit top-3 positions in one week
anchor: SaaS attribution, B2B lead routing, and marketing ops checklist all moved from page 2 to top 3 by week ending 2026-04-07.
connect: All three pages got FAQ sections added in March rewrite.
body: SaaS attribution, B2B lead routing, and marketing ops checklist all moved from page 2 to top 3 by week ending 2026-04-07. All three pages got FAQ sections added in March rewrite.
trace: Cluster of ranking improvements all tied to March FAQ section additions, suggests consistent SEO strategy is working.

title: Comparison hub sessions up 28% in April
anchor: Hub pulled 12,400 sessions in April versus 9,700 in March.
connect: AI Overview presence dropped from 71% to 38% after refresh.
body: Hub pulled 12,400 sessions in April versus 9,700 in March. AI Overview presence dropped from 71% to 38% after refresh.
trace: Comparison hub traffic growth coincides with reduced AI Overview competition in search results.

title: Competitor A comparison page drives 4,200 sessions
anchor: Single comparison page accounts for 34% of total comparison hub traffic in April.
connect: Competitor A battlecard sees 162 opens from sales team.
body: Single comparison page accounts for 34% of total comparison hub traffic in April. Competitor A battlecard sees 162 opens from sales team.
trace: High search interest in Competitor A aligns with sales team battlecard usage, suggesting coordinated competitive focus.

title: Content carries $310K in Q2 pipeline across 6 pieces
anchor: 6 of 18 published pieces show touched-deal flags, concentrated in buyer's guide at $140K.
connect: Gated download on buyer's guide shows 41% week-over-week view growth.
body: 6 of 18 published pieces show touched-deal flags, concentrated in buyer's guide at $140K. Gated download on buyer's guide shows 41% week-over-week view growth.
trace: Content attribution shows concentration in top-performing piece, buyer's guide momentum continuing after gate implementation.

title: Inbound routing SLA compliance hits 95% in April
anchor: 412 of 433 demos routed inside 5-minute threshold versus March at 82%.
connect: Round-robin fallback for after-hours went live 2026-04-06.
body: 412 of 433 demos routed inside 5-minute threshold versus March at 82%. Round-robin fallback for after-hours went live 2026-04-06.
trace: Routing SLA performance improved significantly following after-hours fallback implementation.

title: MQL field completeness jumps to 91% in April
anchor: 678 of 745 MQLs carry complete UTM, channel, and persona data versus March at 73%.
connect: Form-fill scoring threshold deployed 2026-04-08 across 6 forms.
body: 678 of 745 MQLs carry complete UTM, channel, and persona data versus March at 73%. Form-fill scoring threshold deployed 2026-04-08 across 6 forms.
trace: MQL data quality improved substantially after form scoring implementation, better handoff to sales.

title: Marketo-Salesforce mismatch drops to 2.1% in Q2
anchor: 47 of 2,240 sourced opportunities show attribution variance, down from Q1 at 4.8%.
connect: UTM cleanup hit 12 paid campaigns on 2026-04-14.
body: 47 of 2,240 sourced opportunities show attribution variance, down from Q1 at 4.8%. UTM cleanup hit 12 paid campaigns on 2026-04-14.
trace: Attribution accuracy improved significantly in Q2, UTM cleanup campaign contributed to better data integrity.

title: Pipeline attribution coverage reaches 89% in April
anchor: April pipeline with clean attribution chain sits at $2.4M of $2.7M total versus Q1 average at 71%.
connect: Shared attribution mapping with RevOps locked 2026-04-07 for 14 campaign types.
body: April pipeline with clean attribution chain sits at $2.4M of $2.7M total versus Q1 average at 71%. Shared attribution mapping with RevOps locked 2026-04-07 for 14 campaign types.
trace: Attribution coverage jumped substantially after RevOps alignment, better visibility into campaign contribution.

title: Homepage demo form conversion lifts 22% after test

body: CTA test launched 2026-04-04 showing conversion improvement. April inbound demos reach 84 versus March at 61 requests.

body: 480 total opens across battlecard library in April, Competitor A card leads at 162 opens. Competitor A battlecard refresh shipped 2026-04-02.

body: Calculator live for 14 days since 2026-04-08, with 7 deals progressing to proposal stage. Average deal size runs $48K with calculator versus $39K without.

body: Median inbound demo response time down from March at 11.6 minutes. Under-5-minute touches convert to SQL at 2.1x the rate of over-15-minute touches.
trace: Speed-to-lead improvement correlates with routing SLA compliance gains, conversion rate data supports the urgency.
